message:
fix(node): correct lockstep harness log parsing and VEF proof scheduler window alignment

Two independent correctness fixes:

lockstep_harness.rs — Fix log line timestamp stripping:
- Change nested else-if to sequential if-blocks so both bracket-prefix
  stripping ([timestamp]) and bare numeric timestamp stripping can
  apply to the same line. Previously, lines with both a bracket prefix
  AND a subsequent numeric timestamp would only strip the brackets,
  leaving the numeric prefix to be misinterpreted as content.
- Extend numeric timestamp pattern to accept dots (e.g., "1234.567")
  in addition to pure digits, matching real-world timestamp formats
  that include fractional seconds.

proof_scheduler.rs — Fix window alignment index space:
- Convert window alignment to use global entry indices (entry.index)
  instead of slice-local positions when filtering checkpoints. The
  previous code compared checkpoint.end_index (a global index) against
  slice-local start/max_end positions, causing misalignment when the
  entry slice didn't start at index 0.
- Compute slice_end by offsetting from the global start index:
  start + (checkpoint.end_index - global_start) as usize.
- Add first-job admission guard: budget enforcement (compute_millis
  and memory_mib limits) now only triggers after at least one job has
  been admitted (compute_used > 0 || memory_used > 0), ensuring that
  a single job whose resource estimate exceeds the per-tick budget
  can still be scheduled rather than starving indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
